hero.subtitle: HydroRacer is a hydrogen racing boat built from scratch by TalTech students for the Monaco Energy Boat Challenge.
mission.label: Mission
mission.text1: Maritime transport accounts for roughly a billion tonnes of CO₂ per year. HydroRacer proves that a hydrogen fuel cell combined with metal hydride technology and hydrofoils is a viable alternative — not a future dream, but today's technology.
mission.text2: Our project creates a unique centre of excellence in Estonia for developing hydrofoils, and puts Estonian engineering on the map at the Monaco Energy Boat Challenge — the world's most prestigious small-vessel energy race.
mission.goal1.title: We prove
mission.goal1.desc: Hydrogen technology and hydrofoils together — a working prototype, not just a paper vision.
mission.goal2.title: We grow
mission.goal2.desc: Students solve real engineering problems — hands-on, with measurable real-world results.
mission.goal3.title: We invent
mission.goal3.desc: HydroSave metal hydride storage — a patentable solution with applications far beyond sport.
mission.goal4.title: We reach Monaco
mission.goal4.desc: Estonian engineering at the Monaco Energy Boat Challenge — on the world map in 2027.
team.label: Team
team.text1: The maritime sector remains heavily dependent on fossil fuels, even as the world increasingly pursues carbon-free solutions. Estonia, despite being a maritime nation, has not yet fulfilled its potential as a leader in maritime technology innovation at the international level, while young engineering students seek opportunities to work on projects tackling real problems. HydroRacer aims to close these gaps by bringing together ambitious students and complex engineering challenges, thereby shaping the future of maritime technology.
team.text2: HydroRacer is a TalTech student team driven by a passion for engineering, green technologies, and innovation. The project brings together students from electrical engineering, naval architecture, product development, and economics.
subteams.label: Sub-teams
subteams.title: Five specialised teams
subteams.team1.name: Hydrogen Systems
subteams.team1.desc: Developing the HydroSave storage solution based on metal hydride technology.
subteams.team2.name: Electrical Systems
subteams.team2.desc: Designing high- and low-voltage systems and control logic.
subteams.team3.name: Hull & Structure
subteams.team3.desc: Responsible for hull design and composite materials.
subteams.team4.name: Propulsion & Dynamics
subteams.team4.desc: Developing the hydrofoil system and propulsion solution.
subteams.team5.name: Administration
subteams.back.label: Team
tech.label: Technologies
tech.title: Our core technologies
tech.intro: HydroRacer combines three cutting-edge technologies that together create a world-class racing vessel — developed from scratch in Estonia.
tech.card1.name: HydroSave metal hydride
tech.card1.tagline: Safe hydrogen storage at low pressure — patentable solution
tech.card1.desc: Hydrogen is chemically bonded to metal — storage at low pressure, greater volumetric energy density than compressed hydrogen, and safer than liquid hydrogen.
tech.card2.name: Hydrofoils
tech.card2.tagline: Lifts the boat out of the water, reducing drag by up to 80%
tech.card2.desc: Hydrofoils lift the boat out of the water, reducing drag by up to 80%. The technology is new in Estonia — we are developing it ourselves from scratch.
tech.card3.name: Fuel cell + electric motor
tech.card3.tagline: Electricity from hydrogen — the only emission is pure water
tech.card3.desc: Hydrogen is converted to electricity in the fuel cell, which powers the electric motor. The only emission is pure water.
tech.back.label: Technology
monaco.label: Monaco Energy Boat Challenge
monaco.intro: Monaco Energy Boat Challenge brings together the world's leading universities, engineering teams and maritime technology developers. It is not just a race — it is a testing ground for future solutions in real sea conditions.
monaco.text1: Monaco Energy Boat Challenge is the world's most prestigious zero-emission vessel competition, organised by Yacht Club de Monaco since 2014. Every summer, 40–60 teams from around the world converge on Monaco harbour to prove that clean energy works at sea.
monaco.class1.desc: An endurance race where the most energy-efficient boat wins.
monaco.class2.desc: A sprint race measuring maximum performance.
monaco.text2: The competition is more than sport. It is an international validation platform where new maritime technologies get their first real sea trial. The event is closely watched by European maritime and energy sector investors and industry partners.
timeline.label: Timeline
timeline.title: Road to Monaco
timeline.intro: HydroRacer's development follows a clear phase-by-phase logic. From concept to design, from build to testing — every step brings the team closer to the Monaco Energy Boat Challenge 2027 start line.
timeline.step1.title: Concept
timeline.step1.desc: Team formation, initial concept, feasibility analysis
timeline.step2.title: Design phase
timeline.step3.title: Build phase
timeline.step4.title: Season start
timeline.step5.title: Testing
timeline.step6.title: Monaco EBC
partners.label: Partners
partners.cta.title: Become our partner
partners.cta.text: We are looking for companies that share our vision of a clean energy future. Benefits of partnership: brand visibility, access to a talented young engineering community, and the opportunity to shape the future of Estonian maritime technology.
contact.label: Contact
contact.title: Get in touch
contact.intro: Interested in partnership, collaboration, media coverage, or simply want to know more?
contact.location.label: Location
contact.social.label: Social media
contact.taltech.label: TalTech TiVo
contact.card.title: Write to us
contact.card.text: If you'd like to discuss partnership, collaboration, media coverage, or supporting the project, reach out to us by email.
footer.brand.text: Estonia's first hydrogen-powered racing boat. TalTech TiVo student team, heading to Monaco Energy Boat Challenge 2027.
